pi_items.source: Task description strictly requires creating a track named 'Program'.
Task description strictly requires creating a track named 'Ad Break'.
Task description requires splitting at exactly 10.0 seconds. The Ardour session sample rate is 44100 Hz (from setup_ardour.sh). 10.0s * 44100Hz = 441000 samples.
Task description requires resuming the second half at exactly 20.0 seconds. 20.0s * 44100Hz = 882000 samples.
Task description requires the interlude to start at 10.0 seconds. 10.0s * 44100Hz = 441000 samples.
Task description requires the interlude to end at 20.0 seconds. 20.0s * 44100Hz = 882000 samples.
Task description strictly requires setting the volume/gain of the 'Ad Break' track to -6 dB.
Task description requires exporting the final mix to /home/ga/Audio/broadcast/final_mix.wav, which is also checked by export_result.sh.
The Ardour session is configured with a sample rate of 44100 in setup_ardour.sh (ardour-new_session -s 44100) and audio files are resampled to 44100 Hz in install_ardour.sh.
The verifier.py logic explicitly uses a 22050 sample tolerance (0.5 seconds at 44100 Hz).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real public domain audio data (Beethoven's Moonlight Sonata and LibriVox Sun Tzu Art of War narration) downloaded from the Internet Archive. The Ardour session and audio files are explicitly configured with a 44100 Hz sample rate. Based on the task description requirements, the split point (10.0s) and ad start point (10.0s) correctly translate to 441000 samples, while the resume point (20.0s) and ad end point (20.0s) translate to 882000 samples. The required target gain is -6 dB and the export path is strictly '/home/ga/Audio/broadcast/final_mix.wav'. All metadata claims align perfectly with the setup scripts, installer, and verifier logic.
